DECLARATION OF JOSEPH S. MAY IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULTS (TRANSACTION ID # 63133450) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DONG, BETTY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KIMCO REALTY CORPORATION, 280 METRO LP, 280 METRO HC, LLC, MARIA TEOS AND RANFIE ANCELOVICI'S MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULTS ENTERED ON JANUARY 22, 2019 AND JANUARY 25, 2019 IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR RELIEF UNDER THE DISCRETIONARY PROVISION OF CCP 473(B). DEFENDANTS FILED THIS MOTION WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME AFTER ENTRY OF THE DEFAULTS AND HAVE SHOWN THEY WERE ENTERED AS THE RESULT OF MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, AND/OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE DECLARATIONS OF KIMBERLEY HILL AND GLENN BRETTSCHNEIDER SHOW THAT MS. HILL'S SICKNESS AND MISCOMMUNICATION WITH MR. BRETTSCHNEIDER CAUSED THE DEFAULTS TO BE ENTERED. DEFENDANTS ACTED DILIGENTLY TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULTS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE UNTIL APRIL 22, 2019 TO FILE THEIR PROPOSED PLEADINGS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600 EMAIL MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-26-2019 Continued To Jun-26-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jospeh May, Esq., Law Offices of Joseph May, 415-781-3333, appearing Plaintiff;
Jessica Stuart Pliner, Esq., Lewis Brisbois, 415-438-5953, appearing for Defendants Kimko Realty Corporation et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs KIMKO REALTY CORPORATION, 280 METRO, LP, 280 METRO HC, LLC, MARIA TEOS, and RANFIE ANCELOVICI's Motion For Relief From Default

Defendants Kimco Realty Corporation, 280 Metro LP, 280 Metro HC, LLC, Maria Teos and Ranfie Ancelovici's motion to vacate the defaults entered on January 22, 2019 and January 25, 2019 is GRANTED.

Defendants have shown good cause for relief under the discretionary provision of CCP 473(b). Defendants filed this motion within a reasonable time after entry of the defaults and have shown they were entered as the result of mistake, inadvertence, and/or excusable neglect. The declarations of Kimberley Hill and Glenn Brettschneider show that Ms. Hill's sickness and miscommunication with Mr. Brettschneider caused the defaults to be entered. Defendants acted diligently to set aside the defaults. Plaintiff's request for fees is denied. Defendants have until April 22, 2019 to file their proposed pleadings.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
